title: 5 Steps to Get Payment Infrastructure for South Asian Creators Working for Your Store
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Imagine being a South Asian creator with a wealth of digital products to sell, but the financial systems in your region restrict your access to PayPal, Stripe, and other payment processors. You're stuck in a digital desert, unable to collect payments for your ebooks, courses, and artwork. This is the harsh reality for many creators in India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh. But what if there was a way to transcend these geographical and banking limitations and sell your digital products globally? Enter the world of blockchain-based payments and the Core Shop Engine, a backend solution that enables instant automated delivery and global sales. Let's explore the 5 steps to get your payment infrastructure working for your store.
The Full List With Context

Step 1: Choose a reliable payment processor like NOWPayments, which offers instant cryptocurrency settlements and high-security standards. Step 2: Set up the Core Shop Engine, a full backend solution that automates delivery and payment verification, ensuring seamless global sales. Step 3: Connect your wallet with the Web3 Wallet Connect, a frontend solution that enables multi-chain QR codes and live price feeds. Step 4: Configure your admin dashboard to track transactions, manage orders, and gain real-time analytics. Step 5: Integrate the Full Stack Bundle, which includes priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide, to ensure a smooth and scalable payment infrastructure.
Why Each Item on This List Matters

Each component of the payment infrastructure is crucial in ensuring seamless global sales. The payment processor provides instant settlements, while the Core Shop Engine automates delivery and payment verification. The Web3 Wallet Connect enables multi-chain QR codes and live price feeds, making it easy for customers to pay in their preferred cryptocurrency. The admin dashboard provides real-time analytics and order management, while the Full Stack Bundle ensures scalability and support.
The Ones Most Creators Get Wrong

One of the most common mistakes creators make is choosing a payment processor that's not reliable or secure. This can lead to payment delays, lost sales, and a damaged reputation. Additionally, many creators underestimate the importance of automation in their payment infrastructure, leading to manual errors and inefficiencies. By following the 5 steps outlined above, creators can avoid these common pitfalls and set up a robust payment infrastructure that supports their global sales.
South Asian creators can now transcend geographical and banking limitations to sell their digital products globally with blockchain-based payments.
How to Prioritise What to Implement First

When setting up a payment infrastructure, it's essential to prioritize the components that will have the greatest impact on sales. For most creators, this means starting with a reliable payment processor and then implementing the Core Shop Engine. The Web3 Wallet Connect and admin dashboard can be added later, once the core infrastructure is in place. By prioritizing the most critical components, creators can ensure a smooth and scalable payment infrastructure that supports their global sales.
Putting the List Into Action

To put the 5 steps into action, start by setting up a reliable payment processor like NOWPayments. Then, implement the Core Shop Engine, which will automate delivery and payment verification. Next, connect your wallet with the Web3 Wallet Connect and configure your admin dashboard. Finally, integrate the Full Stack Bundle to ensure scalability and support. By following these steps, creators can set up a robust payment infrastructure that supports their global sales and helps them reach a wider audience.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
The Core Shop Engine is the key to instant automated delivery and global sales, making it the perfect solution for creators in restricted countries.
